content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.5-xhigh

## Summary

Found one correctness issue: covered_by_existing_bead reconciliation can still comment on the current bead when br resolves an alias to the active bead. Ran cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --locked -- -D warnings, and cargo test --locked --features test-stub under nix develop; all passed.

## Amendments

1. src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1301 only skips covered_by_existing_bead when the raw target string exactly equals the active bead_id, but the code then resolves the target with br show and posts the comment to detail.id at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1346 without re-checking that the resolved bead is not the active bead. Because this repo supports short/dotted bead aliases elsewhere, a reviewer can provide an alias for the current bead that does not string-match bead_id, and reconciliation will still mutate the current bead, violating the explicit "Do NOT modify the current bead" requirement. Add a post-resolution guard against detail.id matching the active bead, ideally using the same canonical/alias matching semantics used by milestone bead refs, before checking comments or calling comment_bead.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: This is a real scope-safety bug in the new covered_by_existing_bead reconciliation path.
